THOUGHT:
The current reference list effectively covers ML engineering debt (Sculley et al.), distribution shift benchmarks (Koh et al.), shortcut learning/spurious correlations (Geirhos et al.), and benchmark saturation/evaluation brittleness (Recht et al.). However, the "Datasets, models, and optimizers" category remains uncovered, along with explicit support for "Suggesting Future Research" on systematic documentation practices. To fill this critical gap, I need a foundational paper that addresses dataset curation pitfalls, transparency, and how undocumented data characteristics directly cause real-world deployment failures. "Datasheets for Datasets" by Gebru et al. is the most appropriate choice, as it establishes a standardized methodology for dataset documentation, highlights common collection biases, and directly ties dataset opacity to downstream model failures.
